shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The contract sub-item matches the regulation's processor duty to inform the controller of a legal requirement before processing, with the same actor, recipient, object, and timing; the parent clause is separate but does not alter this specific obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both apply when the processor is subject to Union or Member State law requiring processing, and both include the carve-out where informing is prohibited on public-interest grounds.
results.modality_check: Equal force: the contract uses 'shall' for the informing duty, matching the regulation's mandatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'before Processing' matches 'before processing'; no weaker manner, target, location, or cause constraints appear.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient match: processor shall inform controller of intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors
results.condition_check: same condition: in case of general written authorization/authorisation
results.modality_check: mandatory 'shall' on contract side matches regulatory 'shall'
results.other_constraints_check: no conflicting manner/timing/location/cause; added objection/acceptance mechanics do not weaken the notification duty
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The contract requires the Processor to process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller, which matches the regulation's requirement that the processor process personal data on documented instructions from the controller. The parent clause also expressly states that the Agreement constitutes the instructions as of the date of the Agreement, reinforcing the documented-instructions requirement.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are unconditional as to the instruction requirement, and the contract's 'only on documented instructions' is at least as strict as the regulation.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the regulation uses 'shall' and the contract uses 'shall' for the processor's duty; the additional 'may' language about means is separate and does not weaken the instruction restriction.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'only on documented instructions from the Controller' is substantively equivalent to 'on documented instructions from the controller'; the agreement itself constituting the instructions is consistent with documented instructions.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor must allow for and contribute to audits/inspections; contract covers audits including inspections by an external qualified auditor mandated by the Controller, which is substantively the same audit-assistance duty
results.condition_check: compatible: contract adds reasonable qualifiers (during normal business hours, reasonably necessary, confidentiality-bound auditor, purpose limitation) without weakening the audit duty
results.modality_check: compatible: mandatory obligation is present ('to allow for and contribute')
results.other_constraints_check: compatible: 'external qualified auditor mandated by the Controller' fits 'controller or another auditor mandated by the controller'; inspections included; timing/manner restrictions are not looser than the regulation
results.contract_source_text: to allow for and contribute during normal business hours to reasonably necessary audits including inspections, conducted by an external qualified auditor mandated by the Controller, solely for the purpose of fulfilment of the Controller's obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR and for accurately stipulated research questions in this connection provided that such external qualified auditor is subject to and bound by confidentiality obligations as stipulated in clause 12 of this Agreement;

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: both require the processor to ensure authorized persons processing personal data are bound by confidentiality or statutory confidentiality obligations
results.condition_check: no condition in either text
results.modality_check: contract uses mandatory present-tense obligation, equal to regulatory shall
results.other_constraints_check: no differing manner/timing/target/location/cause; substance matches exactly
results.contract_source_text: Processor ensures that persons authorised to process the Personal Data have committed themselves to confidentiality or are under an appropriate statutory obligation of confidentiality.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor remains liable to controller for performance of sub-processor obligations, matching the regulation's full liability duty
results.condition_check: No condition in either text; compatible
results.modality_check: Contract uses 'shall', equal force to regulation's 'shall'; 'remain liable' is not weaker than 'remain fully liable' in this context
results.other_constraints_check: No differing manner/timing/target/location/cause constraints
results.contract_source_text: Processor shall remain liable to the Controller for the performance of its Sub- processor's obligations.

results.core_alignment_check: The actor and action match (processor shall/agrees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures). The object also matches. The contract’s manner clause is broader but substantively covers ensuring GDPR compliance and protection of data subjects’ rights, which includes security appropriate to risk.
results.condition_check: No triggering condition on either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: ‘Agrees to’ imposes a contractual obligation at least as strong as ‘shall’ in this context.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is not looser on timing, target, location, or cause. Its security/compliance commitment is substantively aligned and at least as protective, though framed more broadly.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor agrees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing of the Personal Data will meet the requirements of the GDPR and other applicable national and EU data protection law and ensure the protection of the rights of the Data Subjects.

shared_groups: Data Protection Impact Assessment And Prior Consultation
results.core_alignment_check: processor assists the controller with DPIA compliance and prior consultation of supervisory authorities; same real-world assistance obligation as the regulation
results.condition_check: Compatible: 'where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing' is a reasonable, expected qualifier and not weaker than the regulation's unqualified duty
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract imposes an obligation ('to assist'), not mere permission
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract is at least as specific and broader in scope by also covering prior consultation of supervisory authorities where required, which does not weaken the DPIA assistance duty
results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller in its compliance with an obligation to carry out a data protection impact assessment (“DPIA”) and prior consulting of supervisory authorities where required, cf. articles 35 and 36 of the GDPR;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both sides concern technical/organizational security measures for processing, specifically the ability to restore availability and access to personal data in the event of a physical or technical incident.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered in the event of a physical or technical incident.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses a plain mandatory sub-item within a parent clause that requires the controller and processor to implement appropriate measures; this is at least as strong as the regulation's requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'in a timely manner' matches the regulation's timing; the object and target are the same (restoring availability and access to personal data).
results.contract_source_text: the ability to restore the availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident;

shared_groups: Data Subject Rights And Controller Assistance
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires assistance to the controller in ensuring the security of processing, while the contract clause concerns assistance for the controller's response to data subject rights requests under Chapter III.
results.condition_check: Compatible in the abstract (both are conditional/qualified duties), but the triggering situation is different.
results.modality_check: Shall / mandatory on the contract side, so modality is not weaker.
results.other_constraints_check: The manner and cause differ materially: technical and organisational measures for responding to data subject rights are not the same as assistance in ensuring processing security.
results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller by appropriate technical and organisational measures, insofar as this is possible, for the fulfilment of the Controller's obligation by law to respond to requests for exercising the Data Subjects’ rights laid down in chapter III of the GDPR;

results.core_alignment_check: Same actor and general liability subject matter, but the contract states a broad liability rule under Danish law and clause 13 limits, without the GDPR-specific conditional trigger that liability arises only for processor GDPR breaches or acting outside/contrary to controller instructions.
results.condition_check: Not compatible: the regulation limits liability to two specific fault conditions; the contract instead ties liability to general Danish law and clause 13 limitations.
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory liability statements, so modality is not the issue.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is materially looser/different on the substantive trigger and scope of liability; it does not capture the regulation's specific conditions.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall be liable for damages in accordance with the general rules of Danish law subject to the limitations set out in this clause 13.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to immediately inform the controller when an instruction infringes GDPR or other data protection provisions. The contract only requires the processor to inform the controller before processing when the processor is legally required to process contrary to instructions, and the notice is about that legal requirement, not about an instruction's illegality. The parent clause about processing only on instructions is related but does not itself impose the required notification duty.
results.condition_check: Different trigger: regulation = if an instruction infringes GDPR/data protection law; contract = if the processor is subject to Union or Member State law requiring the processing, with a carve-out if disclosure is prohibited. Not substantially the same condition.
results.modality_check: Both use mandatory 'shall' for the notice duty, so modality is not weaker.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing differs materially: regulation requires immediate notice; contract requires notice before processing, which is not the same trigger and is not clearly equivalent to immediate notice in this context.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the contract parent clause requires the Controller and Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security, and the sub-item specifically includes a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of those measures; this matches the regulation’s requirement to regularly test/assess/evaluate the effectiveness of security measures.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are framed around security of processing and assessing the effectiveness of measures; the contract’s broader risk-based context is consistent with the regulation.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses a mandatory obligation ('shall implement') and the sub-item is part of that mandatory security-measures set.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'regularly' testing/assessing/evaluating is at least as strong as the regulation’s periodic effectiveness-testing requirement; no contradictory timing, manner, target, location, or cause limitations.
results.contract_source_text: a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. 2. In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by Processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed. 3. The Processor hosts the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller via Microsoft Cloud Azure. The online hosting Services are delivered from data centres solely situated within EU, in Ireland and the Netherlands. 4. Microsoft Azure platform provides the security measures as described on www.Processor.com. 5. Particular security measures to be taken out by the Processor under this Agreement are specified in detail on www.Processor.com (Processor's Security and Privacy Protection Policies). Appendix 3 Costs for assistance and audits This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. In the event that Controller requires assistance from the Processor or any of the Processor’s Sub-processors pursuant to this Agreement, such assistance is charged as follows:

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor must notify the controller of a personal data breach after becoming aware of it
results.condition_check: same condition: after becoming aware of a personal data breach
results.modality_check: equal force: 'will notify' imposes an обязательment equivalent to 'shall notify'
results.other_constraints_check: same timing: without undue delay
results.contract_source_text: Processor will notify the Controller without undue delay after becoming aware of a Personal Data Breach.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires a process for regularly testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for security. The contract states that there is "a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing," which directly matches the same obligation, including the required testing/assessment/evaluation of security measures.
results.condition_check: No material condition in the regulation; the contract does not add a conflicting or weaker condition.
results.modality_check: The regulation is framed as a requirement to include such measures/processes. The contract states this as part of the agreement and, in the parent clause, uses mandatory language ('shall') for related security assessment duties, so the force is at least equal.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation requires this to be done regularly; the contract expressly includes a process for testing, assessing and evaluating effectiveness, which is substantively compatible with the regular testing/evaluation requirement. No conflicting timing, manner, target, location, or cause issues.
results.contract_source_text: a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. 2. In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by Processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed. 3. The Processor hosts the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller via Microsoft Cloud Azure. The online hosting Services are delivered from data centres solely situated within EU, in Ireland and the Netherlands. 4. Microsoft Azure platform provides the security measures as described on www.Processor.com. 5. Particular security measures to be taken out by the Processor under this Agreement are specified in detail on www.Processor.com (Processor's Security and Privacy Protection Policies). shared_groups: Data Protection Impact Assessment And Prior Consultation
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: the processor must assist the controller with prior consulting of supervisory authorities; this matches the regulation's processor assistance duty for consulting the supervisory authorities prior to processing, and the clause also covers the related DPIA context.
results.condition_check: Compatible: 'where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing' is a reasonable, not-weaker qualifier and does not contradict the high-risk / mitigation-triggered consultation duty.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses an обязательment-style 'to assist' within the clause, which is sufficiently mandatory in context and not merely permissive.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'prior consulting of supervisory authorities' corresponds substantively to consulting the supervisory authorities prior to processing; the clause is at least as specific on timing and does not weaken the duty.
results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller in its compliance with an obligation to carry out a data protection impact assessment (“DPIA”) and prior consulting of supervisory authorities where required, cf. articles 35 and 36 of the GDPR;

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to ensure that any natural person acting under its authority with access to personal data processes them only on the controller's instructions. The contract requires the processor to process personal data only in accordance with the controller's instructions, including transfers, with a lawful-basis exception and notice duty. This is substantively the same processor-instruction obligation and is at least as strict, covering processing under the processor's authority as part of the processor's own processing duty.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are unconditional instruction-only processing duties, with the contract adding a standard legal-compulsion exception that does not weaken the baseline obligation.
results.modality_check: Compliant: the contract uses 'shall' / mandatory language and is not merely permissive; it imposes an equal-or-greater obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller' matches 'only process them on instructions from the controller'; the added reference to transfers to third countries/international organisations is stricter, not looser.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation allows the processor to process personal data without documented instructions when required by Union or Member State law. The contract instead requires processing only on documented instructions from the controller and says the agreement itself constitutes those instructions; this is the opposite rule and does not cover the legal-exception permission.
results.condition_check: Regulation condition is a legal requirement under Union or Member State law; the contract condition is documented instructions from the controller, which is different and narrower.
results.modality_check: The regulation is permissive/exception-based ('can' without instructions if law requires); the contract is mandatory/restrictive ('shall only on documented instructions'), which does not include the legal exception.
results.other_constraints_check: The manner differs materially: the regulation permits processing without documented instructions in a specific legal-compulsion scenario, while the contract requires documented instructions and does not provide an equivalent carve-out.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object: Processor must ensure persons under its authority with access to personal data only process them on Controller instructions; same obligation as regulation
results.condition_check: Contract adds an exception where processing is required under applicable EU law or national legislation; this is compatible with the regulation's instruction-only rule as a lawful-processing carve-out and not materially looser
results.modality_check: must ensure is equal-or-greater force than shall ensure
results.other_constraints_check: Same timing/target/manner; no weaker constraints. The added legal-requirement exception does not contradict the core duty
results.contract_source_text: The Processor must ensure that the persons performing work for the Processor and who have access to Personal Data, only process such Personal Data as instructed by the Controller, unless processing is required under applicable EU law or national legislation. 13. Liability

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align only partially: the contract notification must describe the nature of the personal data breach, which matches item (a) of the regulation, but it does not address the other required contents (DPO contact details, consequences, mitigation measures)
results.condition_check: No condition in either clause; compatible
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory ('shall'); compatible
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/location/cause constraints; contract is substantively narrower because it covers only one required element and omits the others
results.contract_source_text: The aforementioned notification shall describe the nature of the Personal Data Breach including where possible:

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: regulation requires the processor to notify the controller of a personal data breach; contract requires the controller to notify the processor of possible unauthorized use of log-in information/passwords/credentials or other security breaches related to the Main Agreement
results.condition_check: different trigger: personal data breach vs possible unauthorized use of credentials or other security breaches related to the Main Agreement
results.modality_check: both are mandatory ('shall')
results.other_constraints_check: timing is stricter ('immediately' vs 'without undue delay'), but the subject matter and direction are different
results.contract_source_text: The Controller shall immediately notify the Processor in writing after becoming aware of any possible unauthorised use of log-in information, passwords, credentials or other security breaches related to the Main Agreement. 7. Records of Processing activities

shared_groups: Data Subject Rights And Controller Assistance
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both require the processor to assist the controller with responding to data subject rights requests under Chapter III GDPR.
results.condition_check: Compatible: 'where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing' is a reasonable qualifier and not weaker than the regulation's assistance duty.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses 'shall' and is mandatory.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'by appropriate technical and organisational measures' and 'insofar as this is possible' do not make the duty looser than the regulation; they are consistent with or narrower than the required assistance.
results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller by appropriate technical and organisational measures, insofar as this is possible, for the fulfilment of the Controller's obligation by law to respond to requests for exercising the Data Subjects’ rights laid down in chapter III of the GDPR;

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The contract requires the processor to process personal data only in accordance with the controller's instructions, which is the same substantive obligation as processing on documented instructions from the controller.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both allow an exception where Union or Member State law requires otherwise; the contract also adds the processor must inform the controller before processing unless prohibited, which is stricter.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses a mandatory 'shall' obligation and a restrictive 'only in accordance with' formulation, which meets or exceeds 'shall process on documented instructions'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller' matches 'on documented instructions from the controller'; the added reference to transfers to third countries/international organisations is an included, not weaker, specification.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: The contract addresses processor transfers of personal data to a third country/international organisation and restricts them to controller instructions, which is the same substantive obligation as requiring prior controller authorization for such transfers.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract allows transfers only in accordance with controller instructions, and separately permits transfers when required by applicable Union/Member State law; this does not weaken the prior-authorization restriction for ordinary transfers.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall not transfer ... unless' is restrictive and at least as strong as the regulation's prohibition without prior authorization.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the geographic/recipient target (third country or international organisation) matches, and the contract is at least as strict because it limits transfers to controller instructions rather than merely requiring authorization.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: the contract expressly requires the Controller and the Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, which matches the processor security obligation in substance and is at least as strong
results.condition_check: compatible: both use the same risk-based standard, taking into account state of the art, costs, nature/scope/context/purposes, and the risk to rights and freedoms
results.modality_check: equal-or-greater force: the contract uses 'shall implement', which satisfies the regulatory 'processor shall implement'
results.other_constraints_check: compatible: the contract's 'including inter alia as appropriate' is not weaker than the regulation's open-ended security-measures requirement and includes concrete examples
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both provisions require processor-side processing of personal data to occur only on the controller's instructions. The contract states this directly for the processor's processing of personal data, which covers the regulation's requirement that persons acting under the processor's authority with access to personal data process them only on controller instructions.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract adds the standard GDPR carve-out for processing required by Union or Member State law and an information duty before such processing, which does not weaken the core instruction-only rule.
results.modality_check: Equal or stronger. The contract uses a mandatory 'shall' obligation and a restrictive 'only in accordance with' formulation, which meets the regulation's 'shall ensure' requirement.

Also the contract includes an exception for legal storage, which is compatible.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires technical and organisational measures ensuring security, including confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems and services. The contract parent clause expressly requires the Controller and Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, and sub-item (b) specifically covers the ability to ensure ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services. This is the same substantive obligation, and the contract is at least as strict.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are conditioned on risk/state of the art/nature, scope, context and purposes of processing; the contract adds an appropriate-risk qualifier that matches the regulation's risk-based approach.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract uses 'shall implement', which is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract's 'appropriate technical and organisational measures' and 'including inter alia as appropriate' align with the regulation's 'can include' examples and risk-based security measures; no looser timing, manner, target, location, or cause issue appears.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract adds 'where possible,' which is a reasonable qualifier and does not weaken the duty to include consequences when available.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to make available to the controller information necessary to demonstrate the processor's compliance with Article 28 obligations. The contract instead addresses a narrower, different duty: documenting a sub-processor's compliance and allowing the processor to satisfy that request by referring to audit reports or similar documentation from sub-processors. That is not the same obligation and does not directly provide the processor-compliance information required by Art. 28(3h).
results.condition_check: The contract is triggered only if the Controller requests documentation of a Sub-Processor’s compliance, which is a different and narrower condition than the regulation’s general duty to make available information necessary to demonstrate compliance.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a permissive/defensive formulation ('shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring...') rather than an affirmative duty to make available the required information to the controller.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract focuses on audit reports or similar documentation about sub-processors and even shifts additional reporting costs to the controller; this does not match the regulation’s broader information-availability obligation regarding Article 28 compliance.
results.contract_source_text: If the Controller requests the Processor to document its Sub-Processor’s compliance with the obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR the Processor shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring Controller to the relevant audit reports or similar documentation made available by the respective Sub-Processors with information indicating that the Sub-Processor in question complies with the GDPR, provided that such audit report(s) or similar documentation shall be based on applicable, acknowledged audit standards, e.g. ISAE 3000 or 3402, ISO 27001 or similar standards. Any additional audit reporting or additional other similar documentation requested by the Controller will be at the Controller’s cost and expenses and is charged under the conditions set out in Appendix 3. 11. shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the breach notification to include the measures taken or proposed to mitigate its effects, and the contract parent/sub-item together provide that the notification shall describe the breach including, where possible, the reasonable measures taken or proposed by the Processor to mitigate the consequences of the Personal Data Breach. This is the same substantive obligation, just framed as a description within the notification.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract adds 'where possible' and 'reasonable', which are not weaker than the regulation's requirement to include the measures; they are expected qualifiers and do not remove the duty.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The parent clause uses 'shall' and the sub-item states the measures taken or proposed; together this is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's 'shall include'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. 'Mitigate the consequences/effects' is substantively equivalent. No conflicting timing, manner, target, location, or cause limitations weaken the duty.
results.contract_source_text: reasonable measures taken or proposed by the Processor to mitigate the consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

results.modality_check: mandatory/plain statement is not weaker than shall
results.other_constraints_check: no relevant overlap on manner/timing/target/location/cause

results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to allow for and contribute to audits/inspections conducted by the controller or another auditor mandated by the controller, while the contract only addresses documenting a sub-processor's compliance by referring the controller to audit reports or similar documentation; that is a different, narrower documentation/reporting duty and does not itself grant audit/inspection access or contribution.
results.condition_check: Not applicable / different trigger: the contract is triggered only if the Controller requests documentation of a Sub-Processor's Article 28 compliance, not by audits or inspections conducted by the controller or its mandated auditor.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a conditional entitlement to satisfy a documentation request, not a mandatory obligation to allow for and contribute to audits/inspections.
results.other_constraints_check: The subject matter, target, and manner differ materially: documentation via audit reports from sub-processors versus participation in audits/inspections by the controller or its auditor.
results.contract_source_text: If the Controller requests the Processor to document its Sub-Processor’s compliance with the obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR the Processor shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring Controller to the relevant audit reports or similar documentation made available by the respective Sub-Processors with information indicating that the Sub-Processor in question complies with the GDPR, provided that such audit report(s) or similar documentation shall be based on applicable, acknowledged audit standards, e.g. ISAE 3000 or 3402, ISO 27001 or similar standards. results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires organizational and technical measures including regular testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of security measures. The contract, read with the parent clause, requires the Controller and Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, and the sub-item expressly includes a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. This is the same obligation and is at least as specific.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The regulation is unconditional; the contract adds a risk-based assessment context and a parent clause about taking account of state of the art, costs, nature/scope/context/purposes, and risks. That is not weaker.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The parent clause uses 'shall implement' and the sub-item is part of that mandatory obligation; the sub-item's plain statement is mandatory in context.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract's testing/assessing/evaluating process is regular by nature and directly concerns the security of processing measures, matching the regulation's timing and subject matter without being looser.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to ensure that any natural person acting under its authority with access to personal data processes them only on the controller's instructions. The contract requires the processor to process personal data only in accordance with the controller's instructions, and the parent context confirms this is the processor's duty. This is substantively the same instruction-only processing obligation, and the contract is at least as strict.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract adds an exception where processing may be required by Union or Member State law, which is a standard GDPR-compatible qualifier and does not weaken the instruction-only rule beyond the regulation.
results.modality_check: Equal or stronger: the contract uses 'shall' for the processor obligation, not mere permission.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract's scope covers processing personal data generally, which necessarily includes processing by persons acting under the processor's authority; the additional reference to transfers to third countries/international organisations is narrower/additional and does not conflict.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation allows the processor to process personal data without documented instructions when required by Union or Member State law. The contract says the processor shall process personal data only in accordance with controller instructions, unless required by Union or Member State law to which the processor is subject. This is the same obligation framed restrictively and includes the legal-law exception.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered by a requirement under Union or Member State law.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract's 'shall ... only' is stricter than the regulation's permission and therefore satisfies it.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'without documented instructions' is substantively covered by 'only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller' with an express legal-law exception; the contract is not looser on timing, manner, target, location, or cause.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires that, in case of general written authorization, the controller has the right to object to intended changes concerning addition/replacement of sub-processors after being informed by the processor. The contract gives the processor a duty to inform the controller and says this gives the controller the opportunity to object, but then adds a carve-out: the controller cannot object if the new sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees meeting GDPR requirements. That is materially narrower than an unconditional right to object after notice.
results.condition_check: Same triggering condition: general written authorisation / authorization.
results.modality_check: The regulation grants a right to object; the contract initially recognizes an opportunity to object but then restricts that right in a way the regulation does not permit. This is weaker overall.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing of notice before objection is compatible, but the contract’s limitation on objection rights is contradictory to the regulation’s unconditional right after notification.
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: The contract addresses processor transfers of personal data to a third country/international organisation only under controller instructions, which is the same real-world transfer authorization obligation as the regulation's prohibition on transfers without prior controller authorization.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract allows such transfers only in accordance with controller instructions, which is at least as strict as requiring prior specific or general authorization; the legal-compulsion carve-out is an additional, narrower exception and does not weaken the core rule.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the regulation's 'shall not transfer without authorization' is matched by a restrictive 'only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller' limitation, which is equal-or-greater force.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the subject matter, recipient (third country or international organisation), and transfer context align; the added reference to transfers under law is a narrower exception and does not create a contradiction.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the breach notification to include the nature of the personal data breach, among other items. This is the same notification-content obligation, but the contract only covers one required element and not the full set required by Art. 33(3).
results.condition_check: No material condition in the regulation; the contract's 'where possible' qualifier is compatible and not weaker for the covered item.
results.modality_check: The parent clause is mandatory ('shall describe'); the sub-item is a descriptive statement within that mandatory clause, so modality is sufficient for the covered content.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/location/cause constraint issue for the covered item; however, the contract omits other required notification contents (DPO contact details, consequences, mitigation measures).

results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. results.core_alignment_check: Different obligation: the regulation imposes processor liability for damage caused by processing in specified breach cases, while the contract excludes liability for indirect/consequential/incidental losses arising out of or in connection with the agreement.
results.condition_check: Not aligned: the regulation's condition is limited to non-compliance with processor-directed GDPR obligations or acting outside/contrary to lawful controller instructions; the contract condition is a broad damages exclusion tied to losses arising from the agreement.
results.modality_check: The contract is restrictive about certain damages, but it does not impose the same liability rule or the same conditional liability standard as the regulation.
results.other_constraints_check: Mismatch in object and scope: regulation concerns damage caused by processing and liability only in specified unlawful-processing situations; contract concerns categories of damages and excludes them generally.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall not be liable to pay damages for any indirect, consequential or incidental loss or damages including but not limited to loss of goodwill, loss of expected profit and/or loss of operation, arising out of or in connection with the Agreement.

results.condition_check: Compatible: both are instruction-based, with the contract adding a lawful-processing exception and notice duty
results.modality_check: Equal or stronger: contract uses mandatory 'shall' / 'only in accordance with'
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: contract is at least as strict, including the same instruction limitation and additional transfer-specific restriction; no weaker timing/manner/location/cause
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation prohibits engaging a sub-processor without prior written authorization from the controller, while the contract only addresses imposing data protection obligations on a sub-processor once engaged and does not require prior authorization.
results.condition_check: Different condition: the contract is triggered when the processor engages sub-processors in accordance with Appendix 5, not by the absence/presence of prior specific or general written authorization.
results.modality_check: The contract is mandatory for imposing obligations on sub-processors, but it does not impose the regulatory prohibition on engagement without authorization.
results.other_constraints_check: No substantive match on timing/manner relevant to the authorization requirement; the clause concerns contractual safeguards and guarantees, not prior written authorization.
results.contract_source_text: In the event that Processor engages Sub-processors for carrying out Processing activities on behalf of the Controller in accordance with Appendix 5, the same data protection obligations as set out in this Agreement shall be imposed on that Sub-processor by way of a contract or other legal act under EU or national Member State law, in particular providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of this Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: the regulation prohibits engaging a sub-processor without prior written authorization, while the contract only says the processor remains liable for the sub-processor's performance
results.condition_check: No relevant condition overlap; the contract does not address prior written authorization for engaging a sub-processor.
results.modality_check: The contract's mandatory liability duty is unrelated to the regulation's prohibition/authorization requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching timing, manner, or target constraints on sub-processor appointment are present.
results.contract_source_text: Processor shall remain liable to the Controller for the performance of its Sub- processor's obligations.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation permits the processor to process personal data without documented instructions when required by Union or Member State law. The contract instead requires processing only on documented instructions from the controller and says the agreement itself constitutes those instructions; it does not address the legal-law exception.
results.condition_check: Not aligned: the regulation's trigger is a requirement under Union or Member State law, which is absent from the contract.
results.modality_check: The contract is stricter on documented instructions, but it does not provide the same legal exception; the regulatory permission is not mirrored.
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant timing/manner/target/location/cause overlap that cures the missing legal-law exception.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both sides concern processor/controller technical and organisational security measures for personal data processing, including encryption and maintaining confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience of systems and services.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract uses the same risk-based qualifier ('taking into account the state of the art, costs, nature/scope/context/purposes, and risk'), which is substantively aligned with Article 32(1).
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract imposes a mandatory 'shall implement' obligation, which meets the regulatory requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract includes measures 'including inter alia as appropriate' and specifically covers encryption and ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience; this is substantively within the regulation's listed measures and not weaker.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

(the “Insurance Policy”). In any case the Processor’s liability is limited to the amount that is paid out in that specific case under the Insurance Policy, and if applicable increased by the deductible.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires that organizational and technical measures include a process for regularly testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of security measures. The contract expressly states 'a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing,' which matches the same obligation.
results.condition_check: No special triggering condition is required by the regulation; the contract states the process generally, so there is no weakening condition mismatch.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory sub-item within a parent clause that is part of the agreement, which is sufficient for the required obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing/frequency element 'regularly' is substantively captured by the existence of an ongoing process for testing, assessing and evaluating effectiveness; no contradictory or looser timing is present.
results.contract_source_text: a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. 2. In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by Processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed. 3. The Processor hosts the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller via Microsoft Cloud Azure. The online hosting Services are delivered from data centres solely situated within EU, in Ireland and the Netherlands. 4. Microsoft Azure platform provides the security measures as described on www.Processor.com. 5. Particular security measures to be taken out by the Processor under this Agreement are specified in detail on www.Processor.com (Processor's Security and Privacy Protection Policies). results.core_alignment_check: The regulation permits processing without documented instructions only when required by Union or Member State law. The contract instead imposes the opposite baseline: the processor shall process only on documented instructions from the controller, with the agreement itself constituting those instructions. results.condition_check: The contract condition is documented instructions from the controller; the regulation condition is requirement by Union or Member State law. These are not the same and the contract is materially narrower/different.
results.modality_check: The regulation is a permission/exception ('can process'); the contract is a mandatory restriction ('shall process only on documented instructions'). Although restrictive wording can satisfy a shall, here it does not capture the specific permission to act without instructions when law requires it.
results.other_constraints_check: The manner differs materially: the regulation allows processing without documented instructions in a specific legal circumstance, while the contract requires documented instructions and does not provide the legal-exception carve-out.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to impose Article 28(3)-equivalent obligations on engaged sub-processors by contract or other legal act. The contract clause only defines what a 'Sub-processor' means; it does not impose any obligations on sub-processors.

results.contract_source_text: As part of the Main Agreement the Controller may also choose to use third party integration services and/or applications made available by the Processor on Processor.com in cooperation with the third party providers of such integration services and applications. If the Controller uses such third party integration services and/or applications it hereby authorises the Processor to: i) provide, transmit or transfer the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller to the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application, provided and only to the extent this is necessary for the performance and use by Controller of the said integration services and/or applications, and ii) Process data, including Personal Data, of the Controller that are transferred from the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application to the Controller’s Services with Processor.

This matches the regulation's processor-to-controller notification duty.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered when the processor is required by applicable law to process, with the same carve-out where law prohibits informing the controller on public-interest grounds.
results.modality_check: Equal or stronger: the contract uses 'shall inform,' matching the regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'before Processing' matches 'before processing'; the public-interest prohibition qualifier is substantively the same.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: processor audit cooperation vs making an annual audit report available on the processor’s website; related to audits but not the same obligation because it does not allow/contribute to controller-led inspections or audits on request
results.condition_check: No condition mismatch; both are unconditional
results.modality_check: Shall on the contract side is mandatory, but it mandates a different audit-report disclosure duty rather than audit cooperation
results.other_constraints_check: Timing/manner differ, but the main issue is substantive mismatch: annual website publication is not equivalent to allowing and contributing to audits/inspections by the controller or its auditor
results.contract_source_text: to once a year make an audit report available on the Processor’s website with information indicating that the Processor complies with the Agreement. The report shall be based on applicable, acknowledged audit standards, e.g. ISAE 3000 or 3402, ISO 27001 or similar;

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation is about the processor processing personal data without documented instructions when required by Union or Member State law. The contract clause instead concerns controller-authorized processing/transfers in connection with third-party integration services and applications, which is a different obligation and not the same legal exception.
results.condition_check: Different condition: the contract is triggered only if the Controller uses third-party integration services/applications, not when Union or Member State law requires processing without documented instructions.
results.modality_check: The contract uses authorization/permission from the Controller, not a processor entitlement under law to process absent documented instructions.
results.other_constraints_check: The manner and subject matter differ materially; the clause is about third-party integration data transfers and processing, not processing without documented instructions under a legal requirement.
results.contract_source_text: As part of the Main Agreement the Controller may also choose to use third party integration services and/or applications made available by the Processor on Processor.com in cooperation with the third party providers of such integration services and applications. If the Controller uses such third party integration services and/or applications it hereby authorises the Processor to: i) provide, transmit or transfer the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller to the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application, provided and only to the extent this is necessary for the performance and use by Controller of the said integration services and/or applications, and ii) Process data, including Personal Data, of the Controller that are transferred from the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application to the Controller’s Services with Processor.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to remain fully liable to the controller for sub-processor performance, i.e. the processor bears responsibility for sub-processors' obligations. The contract instead limits the processor's liability for losses/damages caused by the controller's own failures or breaches, which is a different liability allocation and does not address sub-processor liability.
results.condition_check: Not applicable / different condition. The contract conditions liability exclusions on the controller's failures and breaches, not on sub-processor performance.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a restrictive 'shall not be liable' formulation, but it is directed to a different liability scenario and does not impose the required liability.
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant match on timing, manner, target, location, or cause; the cause in the contract is different from the regulation's sub-processor obligation performance.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall not be liable for loss or damages if caused by the Controller’s failure to comply with its obligations according to applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulations or this Agreement. Nor shall the Processor be liable for loss or damages in the event of the Controller’s breach of the Main Agreement and/or other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data, or the Controller’s failure to comply with its obligations towards a supervisory authority, or for penalties imposed by a supervisory authority due to the Controller’s breach or failure to comply.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security appropriate to risk. The contract, read with the parent clause, requires a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the processing, and further refers to particular security measures taken by the processor. This is substantively the same security-measures obligation and is at least as strict.
results.condition_check: No material condition mismatch; both concern security measures assessed against processing-related risk.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory language in the parent/sub-item context and is not merely permissive; it imposes the security-measures/process obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract's security standard is tied to processing risks and the security of personal data, which is compatible with the regulation's risk-based security requirement and is not weaker.
results.contract_source_text: a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. 2. In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by Processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed. 3. The Processor hosts the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller via Microsoft Cloud Azure. The online hosting Services are delivered from data centres solely situated within EU, in Ireland and the Netherlands. 4. Microsoft Azure platform provides the security measures as described on www.Processor.com. 5. Particular security measures to be taken out by the Processor under this Agreement are specified in detail on www.Processor.com (Processor's Security and Privacy Protection Policies). results.other_constraints_check: before Processing is not looser than immediately in this context, and the added carve-out for public-interest secrecy does not weaken the core duty beyond the regulation's own exception

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor/controller to implement or include specific security measures (pseudonymisation, confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience, restoration, testing/evaluation). The contract only says personal data will be processed in accordance with security measures set out in Appendix 2, which is a pointer to another document rather than stating the measures here.
results.condition_check: No substantive condition issue; the contract is unconditional but defers the substance to Appendix 2.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory plain statement, which would be sufficient if the substance were present.
results.other_constraints_check: The main issue is locus/content: this clause does not itself set out the required security measures and instead cross-references Appendix 2. That is a pointer, not content.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation is about the processor being able to demonstrate its own Article 28(1)-(4) guarantees via adherence to an approved code of conduct or certification mechanism. The contract instead concerns documenting a sub-processor’s compliance with Article 28 via audit reports or similar documentation, which is a different obligation and different proof mechanism.
results.condition_check: The contract is conditional on the Controller requesting documentation; the regulation is an ability to demonstrate guarantees, not tied to that specific request condition. This is not the same condition.
results.modality_check: The regulation says the processor can demonstrate through approved codes/certification. The contract says the processor is entitled to fulfil a different documentation obligation by referring to audit reports. This is permissive and not the same substantive modality/obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation’s manner is adherence to an approved code of conduct or approved certification mechanism. The contract’s manner is referral to audit reports or similar documentation based on audit standards, which is a different mechanism and does not match the approved code/certification route.
results.contract_source_text: If the Controller requests the Processor to document its Sub-Processor’s compliance with the obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR the Processor shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring Controller to the relevant audit reports or similar documentation made available by the respective Sub-Processors with information indicating that the Sub-Processor in question complies with the GDPR, provided that such audit report(s) or similar documentation shall be based on applicable, acknowledged audit standards, e.g. ISAE 3000 or 3402, ISO 27001 or similar standards. Any additional audit reporting or additional other similar documentation requested by the Controller will be at the Controller’s cost and expenses and is charged under the conditions set out in Appendix 3. 11. results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires technical and organisational measures to ensure security, including regular testing/assessment/evaluation of their effectiveness. The contract parent clause expressly requires the Controller and Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, and the sub-item adds a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of those measures. This is the same substantive security-measures obligation, with the contract at least as specific and not narrower.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The regulation is a general security-measures requirement; the contract adds the risk-based assessment context and a specific testing/evaluation process, which is compatible and not weaker.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The regulation uses a permissive 'can include' list of measures, while the contract imposes mandatory implementation of appropriate measures and a testing/assessment/evaluation process, which meets or exceeds the requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract’s testing/assessing/evaluating process matches the regulation’s regular testing, assessing and evaluating of effectiveness; the risk-based framing and security objective are aligned, not looser.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of a legal requirement before processing (a notification/communication duty), while the contract only says the processor will use reasonable endeavours to comply with legislative changes and addresses inability to agree on changes to instructions/termination. It does not require informing the controller of the legal requirement.
results.condition_check: The regulation has a specific condition tied to public-interest non-prohibition and timing before processing; the contract has a different condition about failure to agree on reasonable terms for implementing instruction changes and termination. Not compatible.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a reasonable-endeavours compliance clause, not an obligation to inform the controller. Even if mandatory in form, it is directed to compliance with legislative changes, not notification.
results.other_constraints_check: The required timing ('before processing') and recipient ('the controller') are absent from the contract obligation; the contract instead concerns agreement on instruction changes and termination.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.
